page: Amazon Web Services m6id.16xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Intel Xeon 8375C processor. It features 64 virtual CPUs (32 physical cores with hyper-threading) running at 3.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 3800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 61949.91 and a PassMark CPU score of 57701.18. The processor's support for advanced instruction sets is reflected in high scores for PassMark CPU extended instructions (53541.54) and encryption tests (55661.74). Memory performance is robust for cached operations, achieving a read-only small block bandwidth of 3333.76 GB/sec, though uncached large block reads and writes fall into the average range at 167.55 GB/sec and 67.67 GB/sec respectively. The server also demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation with a Clang score of 41672.0 and static web serving throughput of 45.66 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6id.16xlarge offers a dense resource profile by combining high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage with a balanced CPU-to-memory ratio. This configuration eliminates the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput local operations, optimizing resource density. The server is highly suited for database operations, as evidenced by a strong PassMark database score of 26484.23, as well as static web serving, software development CI/CD pipelines, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, running on the Nitro hypervisor with a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The server delivers strong multi-core CPU performance, high cached memory bandwidth, and excellent cryptographic and database execution speeds. With its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-speed local instance storage, the m6id.16xlarge is highly efficient for workloads that require high-speed local data access. It is well-suited for database hosting, software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.16xlarge is a general-purpose server with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core performance and high database throughput.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.16xlarge features 64 Intel vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for databases and general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring high-speed local NVMe storage, strong multi-core processing, and robust database performance.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
256 GB of DDR4 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core CPU and high-speed cached memory performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
